channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Smoke Spams, ZywOo Wallbangs, and the M4A4: How Counter-Strike 2’s Reload Update Might Affect Esports
item.content:encoded:
Counter-Strike’s Guns, Guides, and Games Update has brought drastic changes to how reloading works in the esports title, discarding unused ammo in a discarded magazine.

While the community continues to remonstrate over the update, let’s take a closer look at how the new reload mechanics might affect Counter-Strike‘s esports meta.

Spamming the smokes

While the update was ostensibly to make reloading a more considered action, with advantages and disadvantages, it will likely not affect pro play in the way that it affects a casual player.

After all, pro players already minimise reloads to prevent sound cues, to ensure they are not caught reloading, and they need fewer bullets than the rest of us anyway. After all, 10 bullets may not be enough for you or me to take a site, but it’s enough for Danil ‘donk’ Kryshkovets.

Instead, smoke spamming is most likely the decision that will weigh on the minds of pro players. Whether to give over a precious mag for the sake of maybe hitting someone will be a meta-defining aspect of the change when it hits pro play.

Maps like Nuke or Inferno heavily feature smoke spamming as part of map control and information gathering. Reducing the effect of spam in pro play may be a target of the change, allowing for greater subterfuge at a high level.

For Inferno, Banana control can often be decided by a fortunate headshot through the smoke. On Nuke, Secret crawls can be destroyed by CT spam during the cross.

Prominent wallbang spots, such as the wood spam for the Sandbags boost on Overpass, will also be a consideration for the pros, with more utility perhaps the solution to less bullets to work with.

ZywOo and AWP wallbang spams

Speaking of spam, AWP spamming is a rarer aspect of the game that usually serves a different purpose competitively. Top-level AWPers will rarely aim for smokes; instead, AWPers try to flush out extremely common hiding spots behind wallbangable cover.

An example of this is Mid Doors on Dust 2. CT players will often hide on the other side of the door closest to CT Spawn, making the T-sided player turn the maximum angle to clear it. An AWP shot, or two, can clear out the spot and remove a disadvantage duel for the T side.

In terms of individuals, Mathieu ‘ZywOo’ Herbaut is particularly proficient at hitting those wallbangs, owing to his elite gamesense. The Vitality AWPer spams walls far more than most, especially on Dust 2 and Inferno’s Half Wall position.

Take the example below on Anubis. ZywOo knows that someone is likely Heaven. His teammates are spamming smoke to try to catch the Heaven player and suppress any potential peeks from that area.

ZywOo rattles off a series of shots at the wall, ultimately ending with a wallbang onto The MongolZ’s IGL Garidmagnai ‘bLitz’ Byambasuren.

AWP spamming was already nerfed by the reduction in AWP magazine size from 10 to 5 in 2022.

The latest update further disincentivizes this type of speculative spamming, both because of the reload mechanic and because of the overall reduction in ammo for the AWP from 20 total bullets to 15.

Will M4A4 finally regain predominance?

It may be hard for new fans to remember, but the M4A4 was once the dominant CT-sided gun in CSGO. That picture flipped in September 2021, when Valve buffed the body damage to the M4A1-S.

Combined with the lack of tracers and easier recoil control, the silenced weapon would go on to dominate the meta before clip size nerfs, and a cut to the cost of the M4A4 rebalanced it to a point where the choice came down to individual player preference.

The new weapon system may mark the point at which the balance turns back decisively in the M4A4’s favour. The rifle has 150 total bullets, including its starting 30 ammo and four clips. The M4A1-S has only 80 bullets in total, 20 starting ammo, and three spare mags.

In terms of teams that wish to continue to spam smokes, the result is clear. The M4A4 will allow the CTs to contest those early smokes in a way that is more resource-intensive for the silenced weapon. That is, despite the disadvantage of tracers on the rounds.

The CZ75-Auto, more commonly simply called the CZ, also received a buff, increasing its ammo from one spare magazine to two. The weapon was a menace in early CSGO before a series of nerfs killed the gun in esports play.

The Five-Seven, which took the CZ’s spot in the old loadout system, is still vastly preferred by pro players, but has 60 fewer bullets in the new update, dropping from 100 reverse bullets to only two mags of 20 each.

Shotguns that use shells as their ammo also indirectly received a buff from the update. As each shell is counted individually, there is no penalty for reloading as much as the player desires. The Mag-7 does not benefit, as it uses a magazine.

Shotguns also clear more space in smokes, allowing vision through the volumetric smokes, but don’t expect these off-meta weapons to return.

Ultimately, their effectiveness as weapons is not affected by their bullet capacity, and even if they were good, we are talking about an esports scene that took a full six months to realise that the SG 553 (Krieg) was the most broken gun in the game.

Unfortunately, we will not get to see the effects of the update in Tier 1 until PGL Bucharest 2026, as HLTV has confirmed that BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 will run the full event on the old patch.

In the meantime, do you think the reload update will throw out any meta surprises? You can discuss that on Insider Gaming’s Discord!

For more Counter-Strike news, see how FaZe did in their opening BLAST Open Rotterdam contest.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Ubisoft Closes Down Game Development at Red Storm Entertainment With 105 Jobs Lost
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has ordered Tom Clancy Studio, Red Storm Entertainment, to stop game development, resulting in the dismissal of 105 employees, Insider Gaming has learned.

Ubisoft announced a major restructure at the beginning of this year, which has already led to layoffs, several game cancellations, and studio closures. As a part of the ongoing cost-cutting measures, Ubisoft has announced in an internal email that Red Storm Entertainment will be closing its doors.

Ubisoft’s Decision to End Game Development at the Tom Clancy Studio Leads to 105 Employees Being Laid Off

In an internal announcement, Ubisoft confirmed that game development at Red Storm Entertainment will be shut down, with 105 employees let go. The studio will not be shut down entirely, though, and will continue to provide global IT and Snowdrop support.

Red Storm Entertainment was founded by novelist Tom Clancy in 1996 and is based in North Carolina. It gained significant recognition with the release of Tom Clancy’s Rainbow Six in 1998. Two years later, the company was acquired by Ubisoft and went on to create Tom Clancy’s Ghost Recon in 2001, which received even more acclaim.

Since then, Red Storm has contributed to games like Rocksmith, Far Cry, Tom Clancy’s The Division, and Tom Clancy’s The Division 2, as well as the Mythic Quest TV series on Apple TV+. They were set to be building “Ubisoft’s AAA VR future with Assassin’s Creed” before game development was halted.

What are your thoughts on Ubisoft closing down Red Storm Entertainment’s game development? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Gray Zone Warfare’s 0.4 update is coming soon

MOBILE APP

item.title: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 NPC Locations & Services
item.content:encoded:
A new season has begun in Fortnite with the start of Chapter 7 Season 2, which means a fresh batch of NPCs to locate across the map.

Tweaks to the available NPCs are one of Fortnite‘s customary seasonal changes, often featuring characters that are available to unlock as skins in the Battle Pass, which remains the case this season.

If you’re on the hunt for all the NPCs in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2, Insider Gaming has you covered.

Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 NPC Locations & Services

Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 has a busy map with lots to discover, including a wealth of NPCs scattered into the environment who can be of use during matches.

Continuing the vein from Season 1, not all NPCs have defined services, and their offerings will vary each match. From what’s been seen so far, the available options are:

  • Gain Gold based on your match performance (Free)
  • Spawn a Rift (500 Gold)
  • Restock Ammo (500 Gold)
  • Health & Shields (500 Gold)
  • More Insider Gaming: Should You Choose Team Foundation or Team Ice King?

Meanwhile, Specialists are available to hire who follow you for the remainder of the map, providing support based on their class. This includes ammunition and healing, which can be of major assistance, especially later on in matches. However, they’re not great at hiding, so they may give your position away.

Note: NPC additions and removals, along with tweaks to the provided services, are expected to take place throughout the season.

How Many NPCs in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2?

There are 14 total NPCs available to encounter at the start of Chapter 7 Season 2, which may increase or decrease as the season progresses.

Interacting with each NPC for the first time provides a small amount of XP, so it’s worthwhile tracking them down to provide an early boost to your Battle Pass progression.

  • More Insider Gaming: All New & Unvaulted Weapons in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2

Many of the NPCs this season are located in close proximity, including three at Squibbly Shores, with Fishstick not that much further away at Humble Hills, and three NPCs around New Sanctuary.

Beware, though, as inadvertently damaging an NPC with bullets will make them hostile toward you and attack.

What is your favorite skin in the Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Battle Pass? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, read about Star Wars additions in Fortnite Creative, and check out details on the Italian Brainrot crossover.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Pokémon TCG Pocket Mega Shine Booster Pack Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon TCG Pocket’s next themed Booster Pack, Mega Shine, arrives next week on March 25, 2026.

The most recent Booster Set for Pokémon TCG Pocket was Paldean Wonders, which centered on creatures from Generation 9. This month, the upcoming Booster Set will introduce an exciting new focus: Shiny Mega Pokémon. The Mega Shine expansion will mark the first time players encounter Shiny Mega Pokémon in Pokémon TCG Pocket.

Pokemon TCG Pocket Mega Shine Booster Pack Brings Shiny Mega Pokemon Next Week

A new post by the official Pokémon TCG Pocket account on X (formerly Twitter) confirms the arrival of the Mega Shine expansion in the final week of March at 6:00 p.m. PDT. The developers have released a brand-new trailer showcasing the various Shiny Pokémon and two Shiny Mega Pokémon that players can collect through the new themed booster pack. The spotlight is on Shiny Mega Gengar ex and Mega Charizard X ex. Check out all the cards featured in the Mega Shine trailer below.:

  • Shiny Mega Gengar ex
  • Shiny Mega Charizard X ex
  • Shiny Pineco
  • Shiny Dragonair
  • Shiny Zorua
  • Shiny Ponyta
  • Phione
  • Empoleon

In other news, here are all the crucial details for the release of Chaos Rising in the Pokémon TCG, including the release date, pre-order details, and card list. Additionally, The Pokémon Company has revealed the latest set in the Pokémon TCG, Chaos Rising, featuring Mega Greninja ex. What are your thoughts on the Pokémon TCG Pocket Mega Shine themed Booster Pack? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Curiosity Wakes, the next major update for The Last Caretaker, which finally introduces human contact. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Is Tung Tung Tung Sahur In Fortnite? Italian Brainrot Crossover, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Tung Tung Tung Sahur is one of the many AI characters from Italian Brainrot, and a certain Fortnite marketing video has spawned speculation that the bizarre media is making its way into Epic’s shooter.

Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 1 is finally upon us. A lot of changes are afoot, from the Rivalry Screen to Team Ice King and Team Foundation being a major headache. In the midst of all this, there have been common questions: Where is Tung Tung Tung Sahur, and what on Earth is an Italian Brainrot?!

Is Tung Tung Tung Sahur In Fortnite Right Now?

As of March 19, on Chapter 7 Season 2 launch day, Tung Tung Tung Sahur is not in Fortnite.

The Tung Tung Tung Sahur rumor surfaced when Fortnite appeared to tease Italian Brainrot characters. The ‘Typical day in the life of our Spawn Island Manager’ video attracted a lot of likes and attention, but Sherlock Holmes-esque fans noticed Tung Tung Tung Sahur in it.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 1 Quests

It seemed a strong possibility that the AI character would be in Chapter 7 Season 2, especially from the beginning. However, Insider Gaming can confirm that this is not the case. You need to wait a bit longer.

If you want a first glimpse of Tung Tung Tung Sahur, check out the video below.

When Is Tung Tung Tung Sahur Coming To Fortnite? Release Period, Explained

According to leakers, Tung Tung Tung Sahur is expected to arrive in Fortnite sometime in March or April 2026, during Chapter 7 Season 2.

YouTuber Brani has claimed that Tung Tung Tung Sahur will be available in the Item Shop, and “should arrive in the first two updates of Chapter 7 Season 2.”

Who Is Tung Tung Tung Sahur? Italian Brainrot, Explained

Tung Tung Tung Sahur is an AI-generated character, and one of many in the surrealist ‘Italian Brainrot’ media of AI-created characters.

Italian Brainrot might be one of the best examples of modern-day pop culture. Bizarre phenomena that take off, and Tung Tung Tung Sahur is one of the most popular characters from this trend, alongside Bombardiro Crocodilo and Tralalero Tralala—what is actually happening in 2026?

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Weapons—All New & Unvaulted Weapons

The incoming addition of Tung Tung Tung Sahur has come with a lot of controversy, with creators and developers admonishing Epic for essentially supporting and profiting from AI-generated content. Despite the criticism, it looks like Epic and Fortnite will go ahead with this crossover, and you should have the chance to buy Tung Tung Tung Sahur in the future.

Do you support the idea of having Tung Tung Tung Sahur in Fortnite? Or is it poor taste to support AI content when other designers and developers in the world could be rewarded for their work instead? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out all Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Showdown Challenges & Rewards.

MOBILE APP
